role_delineation_check: The Social Worker is responsible for all vendor logistics, including the selection of the Home Health Agency and the procurement of Durable Medical Equipment, while the Patient Navigator's role is strictly limited to auditing demographics in Atlantis, educating the family on the transition process, and flagging equipment delays to the clinical team.
narrative_summary: An 88-year-old male recovering from bilateral total knee arthroplasty faced a significant discharge barrier when the required hospital bed was not delivered to his residence as scheduled. The patient's family expressed mounting anxiety regarding his ability to safely transition home without the necessary equipment for elevation and mobility. The Patient Navigator acted as a vital liaison by documenting the equipment gap in Atlantis and flagging the safety concern to the Social Worker, ensuring the transition plan remained on hold until the family confirmed the bed was in place.
demographic_audit_note: Audited the facility face sheet against the Atlantis entry; confirmed the patient's date of birth and primary phone number were accurate, but flagged an error in the zip code for the discharge address which was corrected in the system to ensure the HHA referral reached the correct local branch.
home_vs_ltc_goal: Home with 24-hour caregiver support provided by family.
v_card_flyer_status: Delivered the program flyer to the patient's daughter and verified that the V-Card was successfully saved in her mobile device to ensure the incoming Medical Assistant call is recognized as a clinical contact.
pre_dc_pulse_call: Conducted the 24-hour pre-discharge pulse call; the family reported high anxiety (Sentiment Score: 2/5) because the hospital bed had still not arrived. Flagged this anxiety and the equipment failure to the Social Worker in Atlantis and advised the family to wait for the Social Worker's update before finalizing transport.
atlantis_final_sync: Recorded the confirmed discharge date and the first home visit date in Atlantis following the Social Worker's confirmation that the HHA was 'In Place' and the equipment had been delivered.

format_1_state_log.event_description: Patient appeared in the Atlantis queue following a bilateral knee replacement; PN performed the initial demographic audit against the facility records.
PN met with the patient's daughter to explain the post-discharge visit schedule and delivered the Caller ID V-Card tool.
The family notified the PN that the DME vendor delivered a standard walker instead of the ordered hospital bed required for recovery.
PN flagged the equipment discrepancy to the Social Worker and documented the family's refusal to discharge until the bed was present.
Social Worker confirmed the correct bed was delivered; PN verified HHA acceptance in the portal and scheduled the first Medical Assistant visit.

format_2_triples.situation: The patient's family is confused about which agency is providing the hospital bed.
format_2_triples.action_taken: Educated the family on the role of the Social Worker in DME procurement and advised them to direct logistics questions to the facility SW.

format_2_triples.situation: The HHA portal shows the referral is still 'Pending' 24 hours before the scheduled discharge.
format_2_triples.action_taken: Flagged the HHA acceptance stall to the Social Worker in Atlantis and waited for a status update.

format_2_triples.situation: The patient expresses concern about how the Medical Assistant will access the home for the first visit.
format_2_triples.action_taken: Verified the home entry instructions with the daughter and documented the gate code in the Atlantis visit notes.

format_3_rl_scenario.description: Document the DME delivery failure in the Atlantis transition log and wait for the Social Worker to provide a new delivery confirmation from the vendor.
format_3_rl_scenario.rationale: This respects the professional boundary by allowing the Social Worker to handle vendor-specific logistics while keeping the clinical record updated.

format_3_rl_scenario.description: Verify the family's readiness sentiment following the equipment delay and flag the specific safety concerns to the Social Worker to determine if the discharge date needs to be adjusted.
format_3_rl_scenario.rationale: The PN acts as a liaison by identifying the family's anxiety and relaying it to the decision-maker (SW) without interfering in the vendor's operations.

format_3_rl_scenario.description: Contact the DME vendor's intake department directly to investigate the cause of the delivery error and negotiate an expedited drop-off time for the hospital bed.
format_3_rl_scenario.rationale: This is an overstep because it violates the No-Vendor Rule and uses 'Fixer' verbs to resolve a logistical issue that belongs to the Social Worker.
